name: Cheesecake with Cranberry Jewel Topping
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. CRUST: Wrap the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan with aluminum foil. Lightly butter the inside of the pan and set aside.
3. Combine cookie crumbs, sugar, and melted butter. Press the mixture onto the bottom and halfway up the sides of the prepared pan.
4. Bake for 10 minutes on the center rack of the preheated oven. Do not turn off the oven.
5. FILLING: Using an electric mixer, blend all ingredients together. Add eggs and mix just until well combined. Pour the filling into the prepared crust.
6. Bake until the center is set, about 50 minutes. Immediately refrigerate the cake and leave it to chill thoroughly (at least 6 hours or overnight).
7. TOPPING: Combine all ingredients in a heavy medium saucepan. Stir over medium heat until sugar is dissolved. Bring to a boil and continue cooking for 3 minutes.
8. Strain the mixture through a sieve set over a large bowl, pressing firmly with the back of a spoon to force as much liquid through as possible. Spoon the warm topping evenly over the chilled cake.
9. Refrigerate until the topping is set, at least 2 hours. (Can be prepared up to 1 day in advance. Store covered in the refrigerator.)
10. To serve, pipe rosettes of whipped cream around the edges of the cake and garnish with whole cranberries.
